companyDescription:
Vi gör hela Sverige tryggt och säkert!

Att arbeta inom polisen är ett av de finaste uppdrag man kan ha. Du bidrar till samhället genom att göra hela Sverige tryggt och säkert. Oavsett om du jobbar i en civil roll eller som polis, är möjligheterna att växa med en större uppgift många. 

Vi är Sveriges största myndighet. För att klara vårt uppdrag behövs många olika kompetenser. Här finns ett stort antal yrkesroller att utforska, inspireras och utvecklas av. I samarbete med duktiga och engagerade kollegor har du varje dag chansen att göra skillnad lokalt, regionalt, nationellt eller internationellt. Oavsett vilken väg inom polisen du väljer, står vi starka tillsammans.

REGLER FÖR DENNA SIDA
Det här är Polismyndighetens officiella LinkedIn-konto. Syftet är att visa hur det är att jobba hos oss och om polisutbildningen.  Kontot är bemannat vardagar under kontorstid. 

Kommentarer kan inte användas för larm, anmälan eller tips till Polisen. Kontakta oss på 114 14, och vid akuta situationer ring 112. 

Detta innebär även att kommentarer till våra inlägg ska hållas till sakfrågan och hålla en god ton. Alla inlägg i polisens sociala medier blir allmänna handlingar och kan komma att diarieföras. 

Mer information kring vad som gäller i våra sociala medier finns på vår hemsida: https://polisen.se/someregler
companySlogan: Vi gör hela Sverige tryggt och säkert
descriptionHtml:
Arbetsplatsbeskrivning

Vill du vara med att skapa förutsättningar för att jaga cyberkriminella, utveckla nya metoder, verka för ny lagstiftning och arbeta med det senaste inom cyber? Då ska du söka rollen som gruppchef på Swedish Cybercrime Centre på Noa.

Nationella operativa avdelningen (Noa) ansvarar för att stärka hela Polismyndigheten genom att driva utveckling och upprätthålla en strategisk nationell lägesbild över brottsligheten. Vidare samordnar Noa nationellt och internationellt arbete för brottsbekämpande verksamhet samt högspecialiserad och unik polisverksamhet som kompletterar polisregionerna vid behov.

Utredningsenheten vid Noa har till uppgift att ansvara för brottsoffer- och personsäkerhetsverksamheten, utredning av vissa brottstyper som sällan är förekommande eller genom lag eller förordning kräver nationell handläggning. Därutöver ansvarar utredningsenheten för att biträda polisregionerna i den verksamhet regionerna inte har förmåga till på grund av dess komplexitet eller för att den sällan är förekommande.

Nationellt it-brottscentrum (Swedish Cybercrime Centre) är en sektion vid utredningsenheten. Sektionen består av 11 grupper och ansvarar bl a för utredning av komplexa cyberbrott, internetrelaterade sexuella övergrepp mot barn och internationell sexuell exploatering av barn. Sektionen biträder även den brottsbekämpande verksamheten med insamling och hantering av digital bevisning och digitala spår. It-brottscentrum har en bred kompetens samt växande förmåga att hantera ärenden och brott som anses vara principiellt viktiga för polismyndighetens utveckling. Nationellt it-brottscentrum strävar efter att prova nya metoder och tillämpa lagstiftning i syfte att ständigt utveckla vår utredningsverksamhet.

Arbetsbeskrivning

Uppdraget som gruppchef samordningsgruppen för Nationellt it-brottscentrum

Som chef för samordningsgruppen rapporterar du till sektionschef samt ingår i sektionens ledningsgrupp, där ni tillsammans ansvarar för att uppdrag genomförs samt för att driva myndighetens förändrings- och utvecklingsarbete. Gruppen samordnar sektionens strategiska arbete och ansvarar för att driva projekt tillsammans med sektionens övriga grupper. Samordningsgruppen fungerar även som Nationellt it-brottscentrums kansli. Inom ansvarsområdet finns sällan färdiga lösningar och du behöver tillsammans med dina medarbetare söka nya angreppssätt och förslag på lösningar. Samverkan sker även med andra funktioner inom Polismyndigheten, med andra myndigheter och med externa aktörer både nationellt och internationellt. Därför förekommer resor inom tjänsten både nationellt och internationellt. Som gruppchef företräder du hela Polismyndigheten både internt och externt samt arbetar för att stärka förtroendet för polisverksamheten.

Att vara chef och ledare inom Polismyndigheten innebär att skapa trygghet och bygga förtroende genom att utöva ett uppriktigt, modigt och lyhört ledarskap med tillit till andras kompetens och förmåga. Som chef driver du utveckling genom att inspirera och uppmuntra till reflektion och återkoppling på ett sätt som utgår från att vi följer lagen och behandlar människor lika. Du skapar förutsättningar och verkar för goda relationer som bidrar till att vi agerar tillsammans utifrån vad som är bäst för medborgarna och uppdraget som helhet.

Kvalifikationer

Detta är en möjlighet för dig som har:

  • minst sex månaders sammanhängande och aktuell erfarenhet av att ha arbetat som chef eller i likvärdig ledningsfunktion
  • ingått i ledningsforum
  • erfarenhet av att delta i förändrings- och utvecklingsarbeten
  • erfarenhet av polisorganisationens verksamhet
  • polisexamen eller akademisk examen alternativt annan för anställningen likvärdig utbildning kombinerat med relevant erfarenhet
  • goda kunskaper i svenska och engelska, såväl skriftliga som muntliga
  • körkort B
  • svenskt medborgarskap


Vi ser det som meriterande om du utöver detta även har:

  • erfarenhet av att leda andra mot uppsatta mål och resultat
  • erfarenhet av och/eller genuint intresse för teknikområdet
  • erfarenhet av arbete i en internationell kontext
  • erfarenhet av att delta i/driva samverkan med samhällsaktörer utanför polisorganisationen/den egna organisationen
  • chefs- och ledarskapsutbildning för direkt, indirekt och/eller strategisk nivå


När vi anställer kommer vi att lägga stor vikt vid personliga egenskaper och ledarskapsförmågor. Som grund för den bedömningen ligger Polismyndighetens ledarkriterier. Ledarkriterierna anger en beskrivning av ett önskvärt ledarskap inom Polismyndigheten och omfattar följande förmågor:

  • analys och helhetssyn
  • mål och resultat
  • utveckling och förändring
  • social säkerhet
  • muntlig och skriftlig kommunikation
  • skapa delaktighet och motivation


Läs mer om ledarkriterierna https://polisen.se/ledarkriterier/ Om inte länken är klickbar kopiera och klistra in länktexten i din webbläsare.

Som en del i urvalsprocessen kommer även tester att användas.

Kontaktpersoner

Vid frågor kontakta Dana Däversjö, HR-konsult, på 0730-86 97 80 alternativt rekryterande chef Hanna-Sofia Åkerman på 0708-95 13 06.

Fackliga företrädare

Polisförbundet, Sofia Ask, tfn: 010-563 87 10

Saco-S, kontaktas via e-post: saco-s.avdelningarna@polisen.se

SEKO Polisen, Karna Tillheden, tfn: 010-561 66 27

ST, Jessica Stålenkrans, tfn: 010-563 32 66

Övrig information

Anställning

  • Tillsvidareanställning med funktion som chef grupp
  • Anställningen kan komma att inledas med en provanställning
  • Placering Stockholm, Kungsholmen


Ansök genom att bifoga ditt CV (vi vill inte ha något personligt brev) och besvara urvalsfrågorna. Frågorna utgår från kravprofilen för den här rekryteringen och dina svar ligger till grund för det urval som sker i processen. Att besvara frågorna är en förutsättning för att din ansökan ska anses komplett.

Besvara följande urvalsfrågor som en del av din ansökan:

  • Beskriv kortfattat din erfarenhet av direkt ledarskap med fullt personalansvar, dvs driva och leda tillsammans med andra chefer (Arbetsgivare, funktion/roll, antal år).
  • Beskriv kortfattat vilka eventuella ledningsforum du har ingått i
  • Har du erfarenhet av att delta i förändrings- och utvecklingsarbeten? Om ja, beskriv kortfattat
  • Har du erfarenhet av polisorganisationens verksamhet? Om ja, beskriv kort vilka delar, funktioner etc
  • Beskriv din utbildningsbakgrund
  • Beskriv vilka eventuella chefs-och ledarutbildningar du har genomgått
  • Beskriv din nivå i svenska och engelska, såväl i tal som i skrift
  • Har du svenskt medborgarskap? (ja/nej)
  • Har du körkort B? (ja/nej)
  • Har du erfarenhet av och/eller intresse för Nationellt it-brottscentrums teknikområden? Om ja, beskriv kortfattat
  • Har du erfarenhet av arbete i en internationell kontext? Om ja, beskriv kortfattat
  • Har du erfarenhet av att delta i/driva samverkan med samhällsaktörer utanför polisorganisationen/den egna organisationen? Om ja, beskriv kortfattat


Vänligen notera att krav och eventuella meriter ska vara uppfyllda vid ansökningstillfället.

De uppgifter du lämnar i samband med din ansökan kan användas för att kontakta dig, få information och kallelser samt eventuella anställningshandlingar.

Välkommen med din ansökan i form av CV och besvarade urvalsfrågor via e-post till jobb.noa@polisen.se senast den 15 september 2025. Ansökningar till denna befattning kommer endast tas emot via den mailadress som anges i annonsen. Märk din ansökan med referensnummer A533.889/2025 i mailets ärendemening.

Har du skyddade personuppgifter, referenser eller liknande information som du själv bedömer som skyddsvärd rekommenderar vi att du kontaktar ansvarig HR-konsult innan du går vidare med din ansökan.

En anställning hos Polismyndigheten kan komma att inledas med en provanställning om sex månader.

Vi ställer höga krav på våra medarbetares säkerhets- och sekretessmedvetenhet. Säkerhetsprövning med registerkontroll enligt säkerhetsskyddslagen (2018:585) genomförs innan beslut om anställning fattas. Med anställning följer en skyldighet att krigsplaceras. Läs mer om krigsplacering och säkerhetsprövning under https://polisen.se/krigsplacering och https://polisen.se/sakerhetsprovning

Polismyndigheten kommer att behandla dina personuppgifter i enlighet med reglerna i EU:s dataskyddsförordning (2016/679) och annan tillämplig dataskyddsreglering. Läs mer om vår hantering av personuppgifter på https://polisen.se/personuppgifter/rekrytering

Din ansökan är en offentlig handling enligt offentlighetsprincipen.

Polismyndigheten

Polismyndigheten erbjuder dig en arbetsplats som stimulerar till mångfald, kreativitet och personlig utveckling. Läs gärna om våra förmåner som anställd hos polisen på https://polisen.se/erbjudande

Vi bedriver vår verksamhet enligt vår värdegrund: engagemang, effektivitet och tillgänglighet. Som medarbetare inom polisen agerar du för likabehandling. Det är en förutsättning för rättssäkerhet, rättstrygghet och för polisens legitimitet i samhället. Som företrädare för polisen respekterar du allas lika värde och skapar förtroende för polisen.

Läs gärna mer om polisen och vår verksamhet på www.polisen.se
www.polisen.se

Varmt välkommen med din ansökan!

Vi undanber oss samtal och mail från dig som vill sälja annons- och rekryteringstjänster.

companyDescription: Prevent cyber breach and minimize impact
companySlogan: Prevent cyber breach and minimize impact
descriptionHtml:
Do you want to be at the forefront of protecting our society against cyber threats? We are looking for a Senior Security Infrastructure Engineer to team up with the most dedicated team of cyber specialists in our Secure Infrastructure domain.

About Truesec – A Leading Cybersecurity Company

Since 2005, Truesec has been a native cybersecurity company, driven by one single purpose: Creating safety and sustainability in a digital world by preventing cyber breach and minimizing impact.

Over the years, we’ve earned the trust of organizations worldwide and gained a strong reputation internationally. Our team consists of dedicated specialists covering the entire spectrum of cybersecurity – with capabilities in Predict, Prevent, Protect, Respond, and Recover.

Today, we employ around 350 employees – and as cybercrime grows, we need to grow. To support that goal, we are now looking for a Senior Security Infrastructure Engineer who wants to create advanced security solutions in our Secure Infrastructure Domain.

Secure Infrastructure

Looking at the actual threat landscape today, it’s more important than ever to design and build infrastructure solutions that incorporate correct and relevant security features. The team works closely together using their combined knowledge and experience gathered from different projects and Incident Response cases to help our customers stay ahead of the threat actors. We continuously help customers improve their security posture within cloud, data center, endpoint management, identity, M365, and networking.

The Role

The Security engineer interfaces regularly with team members and customers, determine technical requirements, feasibility of projects and expectations. You are responsible for maintaining, implementing, and designing client-facing solutions primarily in Active Directory, Entra ID, Azure and Microsoft 365 Security Solutions. This role is for someone who wants to be hands-on in a Microsoft infrastructure role leaning towards cloud but with the experience and knowledge of On-premises/hybrid environments. You must be able to think on your feet and you’ve got to want to learn and be part of a highly skilled and innovative team.

Other responsibilities you might have can be:

  • Run health and security checks on Active Directory, Microsoft 365 including Entra ID.
  • Assisting our Customers with Tiering Projects.
  • Provide customers with design and also assist with the actual implementation.
  • Implement solutions based on Statement of Work with customers.

Your Potential Background

We believe that your background probably entails 7+ years of experience and expertise within Microsoft solutions for both Cloud and On-premise/hybrid environments. You feel comfortable with guiding stakeholders towards a more secure and robust environment by giving technical recommendations that can persist the current threats present today.

You are comfortable with guiding customers within areas such as: AD-Security, Entra ID-security, Windows Server Hardening, Azure security.

Required Skills:

  • Active Directory Security
  • Windows server Security
  • Azure Security
  • Microsoft 365 Security
  • Overall a strong Cybersecurity competence within Microsoft Infrastructure.

Bonus Skills:

  • Knowledge of scripting languages such as PowerShell and/or Python
  • Cloud migration journeys from On-prem to Azure.
  • Experience with Azure networking: Express Route, Virtual Network & Load Balancers
  • Hypervisor knowledge of Hyper-V, VMware.
  • Experience working with monitoring and logging tools (e.g.: Log Analytics, Azure Monitor, Azure Sentinel, ELK/ElasticSearch/Kibana)
  • Experience with other cloud providers such as AWS and GCP.

Due to the sensitivity in our assignments, we require Swedish citizenship, citizenship in an EU country, or a Swedish work permit.

Join Our Mission Toward a Safe Digital Future

If you truly want to make a difference and make our world a little bit safer, this is the place for you. At Truesec, you'll get to be at the forefront of fighting cybercrime instead of reading about it in the news. This is your chance to be part of an exciting journey in a leading cybersecurity company - and learn from some of the best in their field.

So, are you ready to team up with the most dedicated team of experts and be part of a meaningful mission? Then we are excited to get to know you!

We continuously help customers improve their security posture within cloud, data center, endpoint management, identity, M365, and networking.The Role The Security engineer interfaces regularly with team members and customers, determine technical requirements, feasibility of projects and expectations. You are responsible for maintaining, implementing, and designing client-facing solutions primarily in Active Directory, Entra ID, Azure and Microsoft 365 Security Solutions. This role is for someone who wants to be hands-on in a Microsoft infrastructure role leaning towards cloud but with the experience and knowledge of On-premises/hybrid environments. You must be able to think on your feet and you’ve got to want to learn and be part of a highly skilled and innovative team.Other responsibilities you might have can be:Run health and security checks on Active Directory, Microsoft 365 including Entra ID. Assisting our Customers with Tiering Projects.Provide customers with design and also assist with the actual implementation.Implement solutions based on Statement of Work with customers.Your Potential Background We believe that your background probably entails 7+ years of experience and expertise within Microsoft solutions for both Cloud and On-premise/hybrid environments. You feel comfortable with guiding stakeholders towards a more secure and robust environment by giving technical recommendations that can persist the current threats present today.You are comfortable with guiding customers within areas such as: AD-Security, Entra ID-security, Windows Server Hardening, Azure security.Required Skills:Active Directory SecurityWindows server SecurityAzure SecurityMicrosoft 365 SecurityOverall a strong Cybersecurity competence within Microsoft Infrastructure.Bonus Skills: Knowledge of scripting languages such as PowerShell and/or PythonCloud migration journeys from On-prem to Azure.Experience with Azure networking: Express Route, Virtual Network & Load BalancersHypervisor knowledge of Hyper-V, VMware.Experience working with monitoring and logging tools (e.g.: Log Analytics, Azure Monitor, Azure Sentinel, ELK/ElasticSearch/Kibana)Experience with other cloud providers such as AWS and GCP.Due to the sensitivity in our assignments, we require Swedish citizenship, citizenship in an EU country, or a Swedish work permit.Join Our Mission Toward a Safe Digital Future If you truly want to make a difference and make our world a little bit safer, this is the place for you. descriptionHtml:
Do you want to be at the forefront of protecting our society against cyber threats? We are looking for SailPoint Engineers to team up with the most dedicated team of cyber specialists in our Identity & Access Management domain.

About Truesec – A Leading Cybersecurity Company

Since 2005, Truesec has been a native cybersecurity company, driven by one single purpose: Creating safety and sustainability in a digital world by preventing cyber breach and minimizing impact.

Over the years, we’ve earned the trust of organizations worldwide and gained a strong reputation internationally. Our team consists of dedicated specialists covering the entire spectrum of cybersecurity – with capabilities in Predict, Prevent, Protect, Respond and Recover.

Today, we employ more than 300 employees – and as the demand for cybersecurity grows, we need to grow too. We are looking for SailPoint Engineers to join our Identity & Access Management (IAM) team operating in Denmark.

So, if you want to become an expert in a growing security discipline within one of the leading technologies, alongside a dynamic, ambitious, and welcoming team of specialists, then you might be what we are looking for.

Identity Security Team

Our team of experts consist of engineers, consultants, and architects, and we are here to support our customers to initiate, develop and mature their IAM programs.

As a dedicated SailPoint partner in Denmark, we daily feel responsible for more than 40.000 Identities. We are engaged in- and running several IAM implementation projects, where we work with interesting and challenging use cases for our customers, all spanning from the financial, pharma, public and private sector.

At Truesec, we are a dedicated security house, where we view identity security as an important part to prevent breaches. We believe that collaboration and delivering in teams is key for success. We value a constructive, proactive, and creative mindset and believe that innovation grow when people feel responsible and free to create.

The Role you can own if you join the team

As a SailPoint Engineer, you will join a dedicated and supportive team of identity experts, working together to help our customers implement and maintain cutting-edge SailPoint technologies. You’ll collaborate closely with architects and stakeholders to understand business needs and translate them into robust identity solutions. Whether you’re configuring connectors, designing rules and workflows, or developing custom provisioning logic, you’ll play a key role in delivering secure and efficient identity management for our clients.

Typical tasks could be:

  • Work with architects and stakeholders to understand customer requirements and translate them into technical solutions.
  • Configure and maintain SailPoint connectors, design rules, and Life Cycle Management (LCM) workflows.
  • Develop and maintain provisioning solutions tailored to customer application landscapes.
  • Troubleshoot and resolve issues within SailPoint IdentityIQ and/or Identity Security Cloud environments.
  • Document processes, configurations, and solutions to ensure knowledge sharing and compliance.
  • Support the integration of SailPoint with various platforms and applications. Participate in test and release management for identity solutions.

Your Potential Background

You might come from a variety of backgrounds, such as system administration or consulting, with hands-on experience in implementing SailPoint technologies. Whether you have worked on new projects or maintained existing installations, your technical curiosity and structured approach set you apart. If you enjoy working collaboratively, taking responsibility, and seeing projects through from start to finish, we would love to hear from you.

What would we like to see from you?

  • Hands-on experience with SailPoint IdentityIQ and/or SailPoint Identity Security Cloud.
  • Proficiency in Java and Beanshell scripting.
  • Experience with PowerShell and Log4j2. Familiarity with on-prem environments and the underlying infrastructure required to operate SailPoint solutions.
  • Ability to communicate clearly in English, both orally and in writing.
  • A strong desire to learn and grow within the identity space.

You get bonus points if you tick one or more of these boxes:

While the following skills and experiences are a plus, they are not required—if you are motivated to learn and grow, we encourage you to apply!

  • Previous experience with Active Directory & Entra ID.
  • Knowledge of Azure IaaS & PaaS, Microsoft Graph, and Apache Tomcat.
  • Familiarity with databases and SQL. Experience with SAML, OAuth, LDAP, and authentication protocols.
  • Knowledge of Windows/Linux Server environments.
  • Understanding of REST/SOAP web services, JSON, and SCIM 2.0.
  • Proficiency in Python or other programming languages.
  • SailPoint certifications are highly appreciated.

Due to the sensitivity in our assignments, we require Swedish citizenship, citizenship in an EU country, or a Swedish work permit.Join Our Mission Toward a Safe Digital Future

If you truly want to make a difference and make our world a little bit safer, this is the place for you. At Truesec, you'll get to be at the forefront of fighting cybercrime instead of reading about it in the news. This is your chance to be part of an exciting journey in a leading cybersecurity company - and learn from some of the best in their field.

If you smile after reading this and feel the urge to apply, then we are excited to get to know you!

descriptionHtml:
Do you want to be at the forefront of protecting our society against cyber threats? We are looking for a Senior Security Engineer/Architect to team up with the most dedicated team of cyber specialists in our Managed Security Services.

About Truesec – A Leading Cybersecurity Company

Since 2005, Truesec has been a native cybersecurity company, driven by one single purpose: Creating safety and sustainability in a digital world by preventing cyber breach and minimizing impact.

Over the years, we’ve earned the trust of organizations worldwide and gained a strong reputation internationally. Our team consists of dedicated specialists covering the entire spectrum of cybersecurity – with capabilities in Predict, Prevent, Protect, Respond, and Recover.

Today, we employ around 400 employees – and as cybercrime grows, we need to grow. To support that goal, we are now looking for a Senior Security/Architect who wants to make a difference in our Managed Security Services business unit.

The Role

In this position, you will play a pivotal role in designing and implementing advanced security architectures for some of the Nordics’ most critical organizations. You will take end-to-end responsibility for hardening and safeguarding complex environments, combining strategic vision with hands-on execution.

Your deep understanding of building secure infrastructures (both in theory and practice) will be instrumental in identifying, preventing, and managing sophisticated threats. As a bridge between our detection engineers and solution architects, you will also serve as a vital link to our CTO and Technology organization.

Here, you will have the opportunity to work with cutting-edge technologies and directly influence the security strategy of both Truesec and our clients.

Your Potential Background

You bring over 10 years of experience in cybersecurity, with a proven track record of preventing breaches and minimizing their impact across entire IT environments. You have also developed security architectures for complex organizations. Practical experience from defense authorities or critical infrastructure is highly advantageous. You possess both theoretical and practical expertise in building and securing environments against today’s and tomorrow’s threats. You excel as a technical leader and mentor, and are comfortable collaborating across disciplines to drive security initiatives forward.

Required Skills:

  • Deep technical knowledge, extensive hands-on and strategic experience preventing breaches, including architecture, implementation, and operations.
  • Ability to design and continuously improve security strategies and processes for detection, response, and prevention.
  • Solid experience with security tools and platforms (SIEM, EDR, NDR, etc.) and advanced proficiency in at least one query language (e.g., KQL, SQL).
  • Strong awareness of the threat landscape, attack techniques (MITRE ATT&CK), vulnerabilities, and countermeasures in both IT and OT environments.
  • Ability to work closely with other experts and communicate complex solutions clearly to both technical and non-technical stakeholders.
  • Demonstrated experience leading and executing high-quality, confidential security projects.

Bonus Skills:

  • Experience working with or within defense organizations or critical infrastructure sectors.
  • Experience of Microsoft Security Suite
  • Practical threat hunting and incident response expertise.
  • Proficiency in scripting and automation (e.g., Python, PowerShell).
  • In-depth knowledge of Linux and macOS internals, including system processes, logging, and attack vectors.
  • Experience with cloud security and detection in environments such as AWS, Azure, or GCP.
  • Familiarity with integrating threat intelligence feeds into detection strategies.
  • Exposure to machine learning and behavioral analytics for advanced detection.
  • Experience from delivering managed services is a plus.

Due to the sensitivity in our assignments, we require Swedish citizenship, citizenship in an EU country, or a Swedish work permit.

Join Our Mission Toward a Safe Digital Future

If you truly want to make a difference and make our world a little bit safer, this is the place for you. At Truesec, you'll get to be at the forefront of fighting cybercrime instead of reading about it in the news. This is your chance to be part of an exciting journey in a leading cybersecurity company - and learn from some of the best in their field.

So, are you ready to team up with the most dedicated team of experts and be part of a meaningful mission? Then we are excited to get to know you!

Do you want to be at the forefront of protecting our society against cyber threats? We are looking for a Key account manager to our Sales team.

About Truesec – A Leading Cybersecurity Company

Since 2005, Truesec has been a native cybersecurity company, driven by one single purpose: Creating safety and sustainability in a digital world by preventing cyber breach and minimizing impact.

Over the years, we’ve earned the trust of organizations worldwide and gained a strong reputation internationally. Our team consists of dedicated specialists covering the entire spectrum of cybersecurity – with capabilities in Predict, Prevent, Protect, Respond, and Recover.

Today, we employ around 400 employees – and as cybercrime grows, we need to grow. To support that goal, we are now looking for a Key Account Manager who wants to make a difference.

The Team

The Sales team at Truesec is responsible for new businesses and managing relationships with enterprise clients and serves as the primary point of contact, ensuring client satisfaction and driving business growth through exceptional service and strategic account management.

As a Sales Executive you will be:

  • Respond to incoming business opportunities and inquiries.
  • Initiate and drive new business acquisitions.
  • Support the Sales team to develop and maintain strong relationships with major Enterprise clients, ensuring their needs are met and they are satisfied with Truesec's services.
  • Serve as the main point of contact for client inquiries, concerns, and feedback, providing timely and effective solutions.
  • Gain a deep understanding of clients' business objectives and cybersecurity needs.
  • Identify and capitalize on opportunities
  • Collaborate with the sales team to ensure the timely and successful delivery
  • Monitor and report on sales performance, client satisfaction, and market trends.
  • Prepare and present regular account reviews and reports to both clients and Truesec’s management.
  • Handle any client issues or complaints with professionalism and efficiency.
  • Stay informed about industry trends, emerging threats, and competitive landscape to provide clients with the best possible advice and solutions.
  • Offered a career plan within the sales team

Your Potential Background

Too succeed in this role, we believe that you:

  • Have a strong interest in the cybersecurity industry.
  • Have a proven success as a Sales Executive or in a similar client-facing role, ideally within the cybersecurity sector.
  • Demonstrate exceptional communication and interpersonal skills, fostering strong connections with clients and stakeholders.
  • Possess a proactive, results-oriented attitude and thrives in dynamic, fast-paced environments.
  • Excel in organizational skills with the ability to manage multiple tasks and priorities simultaneously.
  • Have a track record of building and maintaining long-term, trusted client relationships.
  • Tackles challenges with a solution-driven mindset and remains composed under pressure.
  • Are proficient in CRM tools (preferably Hubspot) and skilled in using the Microsoft Office Suite.
  • Exhibit business-level fluency in reading, writing, and speaking both Swedish and English.

Due to the sensitivity in our assignments, we require Swedish citizenship, citizenship in an EU country, or a Swedish work permit.

descriptionHtml:
Do you want to be at the forefront of protecting our society against cyber threats? Are you a seasoned Facilites Manager looking to take your career to the next level? Do you thrive in a dynamic environment where your expertise can make a real impact? If so, we have the perfect opportunity for you!

About Truesec – A Leading Cybersecurity Company

Since 2005, Truesec has been a native cybersecurity company, driven by one single purpose: Creating safety and sustainability in a digital world by preventing cyber breach and minimizing impact.

Over the years, we’ve earned the trust of organizations worldwide and gained a strong reputation internationally. Our team consists of dedicated specialists covering the entire spectrum of cybersecurity – with capabilities in Predict, Prevent, Protect, Respond, and Recover.

Today, we employ around 300+ employees – and as cybercrime grows, we need to grow. To support that goal, we are now looking for a Facilities Manager who wants to make a difference.

Group Functions

Truesec consists of several Business Units who act as partners towards our customers within various different areas of cyber security. The group function supports with e.g. Governance Security and Compliance, Marketing, People & Culture, and Finance and do so both locally and globally. All functions act and collaborate with the organization with a business objective in mind as the tasks are crucial for Truesec to continue to prevent cyber breach and minimize impact.

The Role

As Facilities Manager, you will ensure Truesec’s offices in Stockholm, Malmö, Finland, and Denmark are secure, compliant, and inspiring places where employees enjoy working. You will focus on physical security, supplier and contract management, and planning, while fostering a safe, efficient, and welcoming environment that supports both productivity and well-being.

Your responsibilities will include:

  • Fostering an office environment that prioritizes employee well-being, ensuring it is attractive, functional, and meets Truesec’s security and compliance standards.
  • Managing supplier relationships and service contracts for all facility-related services (cleaning, security, maintenance, utilities), ensuring compliance with company standards and contractual requirements.
  • Developing, documenting, and continuously improving office routines and processes, such as those related to access controls and handling external visitors.
  • Planning and coordinating renovations, office moves, and space utilization to accommodate company growth and evolving work patterns.
  • Overseeing and implementing physical security measures, including management of alarm systems, access controls, and visitor management.
  • Ensuring supplier security requirements are documented and followed in all relevant agreements.
  • Collaborating with the GSC department to ensure all facility operations and supplier relationships meet information security and compliance standards.
  • Managing onboarding and off-boarding processes from a facilities and access management perspective.
  • Receiving, prioritizing, and resolving support tickets and facility-related requests from employees.
  • Managing the reception staff and security guards.

Your Potential Background

We believe you have proven experience in facilities management and supplier/contract management, preferably in a multi-site or international environment. You have a solid understanding of physical security and workplace safety, and are adept at procurement, supplier audits, and service level agreements. You are a strong project manager, planner, and organizer, with excellent communication and negotiation skills. You are fluent in English and Swedish; other Nordic languages are a plus. You collaborate effectively with cross-functional teams, especially GSC, IT, and local office stakeholders, and are committed to creating a positive, inclusive, and productive work environment.

Required Skills

  • Proven experience in facilities management and supplier/contract management, preferably in a multi-site or international environment.
  • Solid understanding of physical security and workplace safety.
  • Experience with procurement, supplier audits, and service level agreements.
  • Strong project management, planning, and organizational skills.
  • Excellent communication and negotiation abilities.
  • Fluency in English and Swedish.

Bonus Skills

  • Fluency in other Nordic languages.
  • Experience managing facilities in a high-security or regulated environment.
  • Familiarity with information security and compliance requirements in a corporate setting.
  • Experience with office expansions, renovations, or moves across multiple countries.
  • Proven experience from working according to SSF200 is a plus.

Due to the sensitivity of our work, we require Swedish citizenship, citizenship in an EU country, or a Swedish work permit.

Join Our Mission Toward a Safe Digital Future

If you truly want to make a difference and make our world a little bit safer, this is the place for you. At Truesec, you’ll get to be at the forefront of fighting cybercrime instead of reading about it in the news. This is your chance to be part of an exciting journey in a leading cybersecurity company – and learn from some of the best in their field.

So, are you ready to team up with the most dedicated team of experts and be part of a meaningful mission? Then we are excited to get to know you!

You will focus on physical security, supplier and contract management, and planning, while fostering a safe, efficient, and welcoming environment that supports both productivity and well-being.Your responsibilities will include:Fostering an office environment that prioritizes employee well-being, ensuring it is attractive, functional, and meets Truesec’s security and compliance standards.Managing supplier relationships and service contracts for all facility-related services (cleaning, security, maintenance, utilities), ensuring compliance with company standards and contractual requirements.Developing, documenting, and continuously improving office routines and processes, such as those related to access controls and handling external visitors.Planning and coordinating renovations, office moves, and space utilization to accommodate company growth and evolving work patterns.Overseeing and implementing physical security measures, including management of alarm systems, access controls, and visitor management.Ensuring supplier security requirements are documented and followed in all relevant agreements.Collaborating with the GSC department to ensure all facility operations and supplier relationships meet information security and compliance standards.Managing onboarding and off-boarding processes from a facilities and access management perspective.Receiving, prioritizing, and resolving support tickets and facility-related requests from employees.Managing the reception staff and security guards.Your Potential BackgroundWe believe you have proven experience in facilities management and supplier/contract management, preferably in a multi-site or international environment. You have a solid understanding of physical security and workplace safety, and are adept at procurement, supplier audits, and service level agreements. You are a strong project manager, planner, and organizer, with excellent communication and negotiation skills. You are fluent in English and Swedish; other Nordic languages are a plus. You collaborate effectively with cross-functional teams, especially GSC, IT, and local office stakeholders, and are committed to creating a positive, inclusive, and productive work environment.Required SkillsProven experience in facilities management and supplier/contract management, preferably in a multi-site or international environment.Solid understanding of physical security and workplace safety.Experience with procurement, supplier audits, and service level agreements.Strong project management, planning, and organizational skills.Excellent communication and negotiation abilities.Fluency in English and Swedish.Bonus SkillsFluency in other Nordic languages.Experience managing facilities in a high-security or regulated environment.Familiarity with information security and compliance requirements in a corporate setting.Experience with office expansions, renovations, or moves across multiple countries.Proven experience from working according to SSF200 is a plus.Due to the sensitivity of our work, we require Swedish citizenship, citizenship in an EU country, or a Swedish work permit.Join Our Mission Toward a Safe Digital FutureIf you truly want to make a difference and make our world a little bit safer, this is the place for you. descriptionHtml:
Do you want to be at the forefront of protecting our society against cyber threats? We are looking for an L2 Analyst – Escalation & Platform Specialist to team up with the most dedicated team of cyber specialists in our Managed Security Services.

About Truesec – A Leading Cybersecurity Company

Since 2005, Truesec has been a native cybersecurity company, driven by one single purpose: Creating safety and sustainability in a digital world by preventing cyber breach and minimizing impact.

Over the years, we’ve earned the trust of organizations worldwide and gained a strong reputation internationally. Our team consists of dedicated specialists covering the entire spectrum of cybersecurity – with capabilities in Predict, Prevent, Protect, Respond, and Recover.

Today, we employ around 400 employees – and as cybercrime grows, we need to grow. To support that goal, we are now looking for an L2 Analyst – Escalation & Platform Specialist, who wants to make a difference in our Managed Security Services business unit.

The Role

As a member of our Detection Solutions team, you’ll take on a hybrid role that combines technical platform ownership, escalation-level analysis, and internal enablement. You’ll play a crucial part in ensuring our EDR platforms are not only running smoothly, but are also leveraged to their fullest potential by the entire SOC team. In this role, you will:

  • Act as a technical platform specialist for Palo Alto Cortex, SentinelOne, and Carbon Black.
  • Continuously monitor, assess, and communicate updates and new features from our EDR vendors to ensure our detection capabilities remain cutting-edge.
  • Enable and educate - sharing your expertise by keeping SOC analysts up to date on the latest platform developments, detection opportunities, and best practices. Translate complex updates into actionable guidance and ensure knowledge is embedded in our processes.
  • Support and mentor SOC analysts during escalations, acting as the second line of defence in complex investigations.
  • Work closely with platform vendors, engineering teams, and internal stakeholders to troubleshoot issues, optimize deployments and increase detection capabilities, representing the Detection Solutions team with clarity and credibility.
  • Drive ongoing enhancements across our detection tooling and processes, ensuring that new platform capabilities are rapidly and effectively integrated.

Your Potential Background

We are looking for someone with a passion for cybersecurity, a strong sense of product ownership, and a collaborative mindset. You thrive in a dynamic environment, are eager to stay current with the latest technologies, and enjoy sharing your expertise with others. You have 2-5 years of hands-on experience in cybersecurity, ideally within a SOC environment or a similar operational role.

Required Skills:

  • Familiarity with EDR platforms, particularly advantageous if it’s Palo Alto Cortex XDR, SentinelOne, or Carbon Black.
  • A good understanding of the difference in operational context between cloud-native vs. on-premises EDR tools.
  • A strong interest in product ownership, with a proactive approach to learning and staying current with evolving platform features and best practices.
  • Ability to communicate complex technical concepts to both technical and non-technical audiences.
  • Collaborative and outgoing personality; comfortable representing the team in cross-functional meetings, vendor calls, and technical customer discussions.
  • Self-motivated, with a proven ability to work independently and take ownership of responsibilities.
  • Bonus points if you have experience writing detection rules or working with API integrations in EDR platforms.

Due to the sensitivity in our assignments, we require Swedish citizenship, citizenship in an EU country, or a Swedish work permit. 

Join Our Mission Toward a Safe Digital Future

If you truly want to make a difference and make our world a little bit safer, this is the place for you. At Truesec, you’ll get to be at the forefront of fighting cybercrime instead of reading about it in the news. This is your chance to be part of an exciting journey in a leading cybersecurity company – and learn from some of the best in their field.

So, are you ready to team up with the most dedicated team of experts and be part of a meaningful mission? Then we are excited to get to know you!

descriptionHtml:
Do you want to be at the forefront of protecting our society against cyber threats? We are looking for Senior Advisors to team up with the most dedicated Strategic Cyber Advisors in our Cyber Strategy & Transformation team in Stockholm.

About Truesec – A Leading Cybersecurity Company

Since 2005, Truesec has been a native cybersecurity company, driven by one single purpose: Creating safety and sustainability in a digital world by preventing cyber breach and minimizing impact.

Over the years, we’ve earned the trust of organizations worldwide and gained a strong reputation internationally. Our team consists of dedicated specialists covering the entire spectrum of cybersecurity – with capabilities in Predict, Prevent, Protect, Respond, and Recover.

Today, we employ around 350 employees – and as cybercrime grows, we need to grow. To support that goal, we are now looking for a Strategic Advisor who wants to make a difference in our Cyber Strategy & Transformation team.

Cyber Strategy & Transformation

Within Cyber Strategy & Transformation we systematically help our clients build effective strategic cyber security journeys to a suitable target state. This through designing prioritised strategic roadmaps based on the threat landscape and the client´s business, including legal & regulatory landscape and current cyber security capabilities as well as current and necessary spend. Based on the roadmap we support the CISO/CSO, Management Teams and Board of directors on the journey, optimally through formal transformation programs, updating their cyber game with the necessary cyber capabilities. On top of this we can augment the clients´existing operating models with the experience and skills necessary for the shaping and building and to the extent necessary as part of the programs.

We bring a truly holistic approach covering the threat dimension, business dimension and capability dimension and we know how to communicate complex matters related to cyber security and cyber risk with the C-suite. We dedicate ourselves to secure digital business transformation for a modern digital world. Our strategic cyber advisors have backgrounds in military intelligence operations, strategic cyber advisory practice, IT governance, cyber assurance and overall GRC implementation. We support C-Suite decision makers in Management Teams and Boards of Directors in shaping decisions based on experience and actionable insights. With decades of industry experience and business risk insights we support our clients´ to achieve their business strategies and objectives. Many of our advisors have been CSOs/CISOs previously in their careers and have good understanding of the expectations on C-Suite stakeholders when it comes to cyber security.

The Roles

As a Senior Advisor you have broad knowledge and experience of cyber security and is a subject matter expert in several relevant cyber security domains. Further you have good knowledge about the enterprise context and experience from working with C-Suite stakeholders. You also have the ability to translate client needs to sales opportunities to deliver upon.

You will:

  • provide strategic cyber advice and insights
  • act as Engagement Manager in relation to client engagements
  • be part of the sales process as an advisor to the client and the account team
  • holistically design cyber capabilities and support the client in the implementation

Your Background

You have relevant experience working in the industry, including big four, professional services firms or in a C-Suite position. You have a good orientation in the cyber threat, business, and cyber capability dimensions.

Required Skills:

  • You are a skilled communicator both verbally and written
  • You have broad knowledge and experience of cyber security and is a subject matter expert in several relevant cyber security domains
  • You have knowledge and experience of security principles, cyber security frameworks and standards [NIST CSF, ISO/IEC 27001/2, CIS, CSA CCM etc.] and risk management practice
  • You have knowledge and experience of applicable cyber security and data privacy regulations [NIS2, DORA, GDPR etc.] and compliance management
  • You are fluent in Swedish and English both verbally and written

Bonus Skills:

  • You have experience from strategy & transformation work, including strategy & policy, program and project management and capability design & architetcure
  • You have relevant certifications such as CISSP, CISM, TOGAF
  • You have knowledge and experience of IT governance and ITSM [COBIT, ITIL]
  • You are fluent in Swedish both verbally and written

Due to the sensitivity in our assignments, we require Swedish citizenship, citizenship in an EU country, or a Swedish work permit.

Join Our Mission Toward a Safe Digital Future

If you truly want to make a difference and make our world a little bit safer, this is the place for you. At Truesec, you'll get to be at the forefront of fighting cybercrime instead of reading about it in the news. This is your chance to be part of an exciting journey in a leading cybersecurity company - and learn from some of the best in their field.

So, are you ready to team up with the most dedicated team of experts and be part of a meaningful mission? Then we are excited to get to know you!

descriptionHtml:
Truesec is the leading Nordic Cyber Security company, striving to become a European Leader in our industry. Technology will be playing an absolutely central role in how we continue to grow the company, and we are therefore looking for a growth-oriented and inspiring CTO who can lead our Technology team on this journey.

Today we offer a wide range of services, but the direction is clear. We will significantly increase our use of AI, our in-house platform’s role will be key for our continued growth, we will continue to find the best use cases for automation, and we need to accelerate how we bring best of breed Technology to all teams in our organization.

About Truesec – A Leading Cybersecurity Company

Since 2005, Truesec has been the leading Nordic Cyber Security company, driven by one single purpose: Creating safety and sustainability in a digital world by preventing cyber breaches and minimizing impact. Our team consists of dedicated specialists covering the entire spectrum of cybersecurity – with capabilities in Predict, Prevent, Protect, Respond, and Recover.

About The Technology Organization

This technology business unit at Truesec brings together highly skilled engineers, architects, platform specialists and IT specialist with a mission to develop, operate, and continuously improve our unified Truesec Platform and technical capabilities. The team drives the adoption of AI, automation, and advanced technology across all business units, ensuring we deliver best-in-class cybersecurity solutions to our clients and maintain a robust, scalable, and secure technology stack.

As CTO, you will lead and shape this dynamic organization—empowering experts, fostering cross-team collaboration, and ensuring that our technology enables Truesec’s ambition to be the leading European cyber partner. The Technology organization works closely with all parts of Truesec, serving as a key enabler for innovation, operational excellence, and customer value.

The Role

As CTO, you will play a key role in Truesec’s continued growth journey. The role combines executive management, thought leadership, and people management, and will be pivotal as we increase our use of AI, expand our in-house platform, and accelerate the adoption of best-in-breed technology across all teams.

Key Responsibilities

  • Define and execute the company’s technology vision and roadmap in alignment with Truesec’s strategic direction and service/product portfolio.
  • Lead the Technology team and foster a culture of innovation, technical excellence, and continuous improvement.
  • Drive the development and improvement of our in-house platform, ensuring it is a key enabler for growth.
  • Significantly increase our use of AI and automation, identifying and implementing the best use cases across the organization.
  • Ensure that Truesec remains at the forefront of cybersecurity technology and best practices.
  • Collaborate with business units to support international expansion and scalable service delivery.
  • Represent Truesec externally as a technology thought leader at industry events and in published content.
  • Establish and maintain secure system architecture in collaboration with GC (Global Complance) to ensure technology meets all statutory, regulatory, and contractual obligations.

Your Potential Background

  • Proven experience in a senior technology leadership role, preferably as CTO, in cybersecurity or related fields.
  • Demonstrated success in platform development and scaling technology organizations, ideally with cloud and AI/ML experience.
  • Deep understanding of cloud-native application design, DevOps practices, and scalable architecture.
  • Strong expertise in AI, automation, and cybersecurity.
  • Experience building and scaling technology teams and engineering organizations

Required Skills

  • Executive leadership in technology organizations
  • Strategic vision and roadmap execution
  • Experience with AI and automation in business-critical environments
  • Strong people leadership and communication skills
  • Experience from international, high-growth environments

Due to the sensitivity of our assignments, we require Swedish/Danish/Finnish citizenship, citizenship in an EU country, or a Swedish/Danish/Finnish work permit.

Join Our Mission Toward a Safe Digital Future

If you truly want to make a difference and make our world a little bit safer, this is the place for you. At Truesec, you’ll get to be at the forefront of fighting cybercrime instead of reading about it in the news. This is your chance to be part of an exciting journey in a leading cybersecurity company – and learn from some of the best in their field.

Are you ready to team up with the most dedicated team of experts and be part of a meaningful mission? Then we are excited to get to know you!

descriptionHtml:
Do you want to contribute to protecting society against cyber threats? We are looking for an Identity Security Consultant to join the most supportive team in the Identity & Access Management domain, where your unique skills and perspectives will be valued.

About Truesec – A Leading Cybersecurity Company

Since 2005, Truesec has been a native cybersecurity company, driven by one single purpose: Creating safety and sustainability in a digital world by preventing cyber breach and minimizing impact.

Over the years, we’ve earned the trust of organizations worldwide and gained a strong reputation internationally. Our team consists of dedicated specialists covering the entire spectrum of cybersecurity – with capabilities in Predict, Prevent, Protect, Respond, and Recover.

Today, we employ around 300 employees – and as the demand for cybersecurity expertise grows we need to grow too. So, we are now looking for a new colleague who wants to enhance on their skills, with focus on SailPoint and Microsoft technology, to join our team.

So, if you want to become an expert in a growing security discipline within one of the leading technologies, alongside a dynamic, ambitious, and welcoming team of specialists, then you might be what we are looking for.

Identity Security team

Our team of experts includes professionals with diverse skill sets: engineers, consultants, architects, process specialists, and strategists. Together, we support our customers to prepare, initiate, develop and mature their IAM programs.

As a dedicated SailPoint partner in the Nordics, we take pride in contributing to the security and management of over 40,000 identities.

Our culture is built on collaboration, curiosity, and continuous learning. You will be surrounded by experts who support and challenge each other to grow, whether through knowledge-sharing, hands-on problem-solving, or staying ahead of the latest identity threats. We believe that diverse perspectives and experiences make us stronger, which is why we value team members from different backgrounds, industries, and walks of life.

The role you can own if you join the team

As a consultant within IAM, you will have the opportunity to collaborate with a supportive team to implement IAM Technologies and maintain customer solutions. We work with both people, processes & technologies and have a variety of challenging assignments that will develop your consultant skills, both if you seek technical challenges or prefer to be more on the business side as a business consultant.

Typical tasks could be:

  • Work with stakeholders to understand business processes and requirements, translating and re-designing these together with developers and architects.
  • Coordinate tasks across internal and customer IT teams to ensure timely deliverables.
  • Analyze and document IAM processes, data mappings, and user stories.
  • Perform maturity analyses and create IAM strategies and roadmaps.
  • Assess and ensure regulatory compliance.
  • Perform Test and Release Management.

Your Potential Background

You can come with many backgrounds, whether you have a formal degree in business or IT, or hands-on experience in e.g. business strategy, project management, or risk analysis. If you have strong analytical skills, are a great communicator, and thrive in collaborative environments, we want to hear from you.

What would we like to see from you?

  • You have hands-on working experience within the cyber or identity space.
  • You know how to collaborate, communicate, and present.
  • You are able to translate complex concepts into actionable recommendations.
  • You are highly motivated to learn.
  • You can communicate clearly in English both orally and in writing.

You get bonus points if you tick one or more of these boxes:

While the following skills and experiences are a plus, they are not required—if you are motivated to learn and grow, we encourage you to apply!

  • Previous experience within Identity & Access Management, business consulting, strategy, risk management.
  • Knowledge of IAM technologies, especially Entra ID Governance and SailPoint
  • Project / task management experience
  • Familiarity with process mapping, requirements analysis, regulatory compliance mapping

Due to the sensitivity in our assignments, we require Swedish citizenship, citizenship in an EU country, or a Swedish work permit.

Join Our Mission Toward a Safe Digital Future

If you truly want to make a difference and make our world a little bit safer, this is the place for you. At Truesec, you'll get to be at the forefront of fighting cybercrime instead of reading about it in the news. This is your chance to be part of an exciting journey in a leading international cybersecurity company - and learn from some of the best in their field.

If you smile after reading this and feel the urge to apply, then we are excited to get to know you!

What is Identity and Access Management?

Identity and Access Management (IAM) is a critical area of cybersecurity that ensures the right people have access to the right information at the right time.

By safeguarding user credentials and access privileges, IAM helps organizations protect sensitive data and reduce the risk of security breaches. Join us in making a difference in this growing field!

So, we are now looking for a new colleague who wants to enhance on their skills, with focus on SailPoint and Microsoft technology, to join our team.So, if you want to become an expert in a growing security discipline within one of the leading technologies, alongside a dynamic, ambitious, and welcoming team of specialists, then you might be what we are looking for.Identity Security team Our team of experts includes professionals with diverse skill sets: engineers, consultants, architects, process specialists, and strategists. Together, we support our customers to prepare, initiate, develop and mature their IAM programs.As a dedicated SailPoint partner in the Nordics, we take pride in contributing to the security and management of over 40,000 identities.Our culture is built on collaboration, curiosity, and continuous learning. You will be surrounded by experts who support and challenge each other to grow, whether through knowledge-sharing, hands-on problem-solving, or staying ahead of the latest identity threats. We believe that diverse perspectives and experiences make us stronger, which is why we value team members from different backgrounds, industries, and walks of life.The role you can own if you join the team As a consultant within IAM, you will have the opportunity to collaborate with a supportive team to implement IAM Technologies and maintain customer solutions. We work with both people, processes & technologies and have a variety of challenging assignments that will develop your consultant skills, both if you seek technical challenges or prefer to be more on the business side as a business consultant.Typical tasks could be: Work with stakeholders to understand business processes and requirements, translating and re-designing these together with developers and architects. Coordinate tasks across internal and customer IT teams to ensure timely deliverables. Analyze and document IAM processes, data mappings, and user stories. Perform maturity analyses and create IAM strategies and roadmaps. Assess and ensure regulatory compliance. Perform Test and Release Management. Your Potential Background You can come with many backgrounds, whether you have a formal degree in business or IT, or hands-on experience in e.g. business strategy, project management, or risk analysis. If you have strong analytical skills, are a great communicator, and thrive in collaborative environments, we want to hear from you.What would we like to see from you? You have hands-on working experience within the cyber or identity space. You know how to collaborate, communicate, and present. You are able to translate complex concepts into actionable recommendations. You are highly motivated to learn. You can communicate clearly in English both orally and in writing. You get bonus points if you tick one or more of these boxes: While the following skills and experiences are a plus, they are not required—if you are motivated to learn and grow, we encourage you to apply!Previous experience within Identity & Access Management, business consulting, strategy, risk management. Knowledge of IAM technologies, especially Entra ID Governance and SailPoint Project / task management experience Familiarity with process mapping, requirements analysis, regulatory compliance mapping Due to the sensitivity in our assignments, we require Swedish citizenship, citizenship in an EU country, or a Swedish work permit. Join Our Mission Toward a Safe Digital Future If you truly want to make a difference and make our world a little bit safer, this is the place for you. At Truesec, you'll get to be at the forefront of fighting cybercrime instead of reading about it in the news. This is your chance to be part of an exciting journey in a leading international cybersecurity company - and learn from some of the best in their field.If you smile after reading this and feel the urge to apply, then we are excited to get to know you!What is Identity and Access Management? Identity and Access Management (IAM) is a critical area of cybersecurity that ensures the right people have access to the right information at the right time.By safeguarding user credentials and access privileges, IAM helps organizations protect sensitive data and reduce the risk of security breaches. Join us in making a difference in this growing field!

descriptionHtml:
Do you want to be at the forefront of protecting our society against cyber threats? We are looking for cybersecurity advisor to team up with the most dedicated team of cyber specialists in our Security Strategy domain.

About Truesec – A Leading Cybersecurity Company

Since 2005, Truesec has been a native cybersecurity company, driven by one single purpose: Creating safety and sustainability in a digital world by preventing cyber breach and minimizing impact.

Over the years, we’ve earned the trust of organisations worldwide and gained a strong reputation internationally. Our team consists of dedicated specialists covering the entire spectrum of cybersecurity – with capabilities in Identify, Protect, Detect, Respond, Recover and Governance.

Today, we employ around 300 employees – and as cybercrime grows, we need to grow. To support that goal, we are now looking for a Cybersecurity Advisor who can make a difference in our Security Strategy Team.

Security Advisory

As a trusted cybersecurity advisor, we help our clients strengthen their cyber defence capabilities by developing end-to-end cybersecurity strategies & roadmaps tailored to their needs. Given our insight based on firsthand experience of data breaches and cybercrime, we know what is required to protect today's modern digital environments. We guide our clients, both strategically and tactically along the way, providing them with reliable security guidance and advisory to ensure business continuity and to reduce their cyber risk.

The Role

We are looking for a cybersecurity professional that understands how to advice and guide clients through the fog of cybersecurity threats, tools, frameworks and legislation requirements. You are someone who enjoys taking security strategy and making it work for the business, and are passionate about making a real difference for the organization.

You combine cybersecurity, risk analysis and business understanding to improve cybersecurity posture for our clients – not only on paper, but IRL.

Your Potential Background

You ideally have 4-6 years of experience working in an cybersecurity advisory/consultancy capacity and possess a large client network in Denmark. You are well versed in delivering & communicating cybersecurity gaps and cybersecurity roadmaps to both technical- & business profiles and higher management such as executive boards. You are also strong in identifying and developing future cybersecurity initiatives and services for Truesec and our clients.

Required Skills:

  • Experienced in Security frameworks (CIS v8, NIST CSF, ISO27001 etc.)
  • Hands-on cybersecurity assessment using security frameworks
  • Experienced in developing and communicating cybersecurity findings and cyber security roadmaps
  • Experience in presenting cybersecurity findings and risk assessments to c-level and executive boards
  • Knowledgeable of NIS2 and DORA
  • Knowledgeable about infrastructure; for instance AD, EntraID, MS E5 tools, IGA, PAM and SOC
  • You have great collaboration, communication and presentation skills (PowerPoint) and you can communicate clearly in Danish and English both orally and in writing.
  • You have excellent Office365 (PowerPoint, Excel & Teams) skills
  • You are a team player, and you find it natural to share your knowledge with your colleagues

Bonus Skills:

  • Relevant certifications CISM, CISSP, CCSP, ISO2700x etc. is seen as an advantage
  • Basic knowledge of infrastructure, AD, Azure, IGA, PAM and SOC is seen as an advantage.
  • Knowledge in Incident Response

Due to the sensitivity in our assignments, we require Swedish citizenship, citizenship in an EU country, or a Swedish work permit.

Join Our Mission Toward a Safe Digital Future

If you truly want to make a difference and make our world a little bit safer, this is the place for you. At Truesec, you'll get to be at the forefront of fighting cybercrime instead of reading about it in the news. This is your chance to be part of an exciting journey in a leading cybersecurity company - and learn from some of the best in their field.

So, are you ready to team up with the most dedicated team of experts and be part of a meaningful mission? Then we are excited to get to know you!

descriptionHtml:
Do you want to be at the forefront of protecting our society against cyber threats? We are looking for a Head of Cyber Threat Intelligence to team up with the most dedicated team of cybersecurity specialists at Truesec.

About Truesec – A Leading Cybersecurity Company

Since 2005, Truesec has been a native cybersecurity company, driven by one single purpose: Creating safety and sustainability in a digital world by preventing cyber breaches and minimizing their impact. Over the years, we’ve earned the trust of organizations worldwide and gained a strong reputation internationally.

Our team consists of dedicated specialists covering the entire spectrum of cybersecurity – with capabilities in Predict, Prevent, Protect, Respond, and Recover. Today, we employ around 350 employees, and as cybercrime grows, so do we. To support that goal, we are now looking for a Head of Cyber Threat Intelligence who wants to make a difference together with us at Truesec.

About Truesec Cyber Threat Intelligence Team

Our Cyber Threat Intelligence team is a critical part of Truesec’s mission to protect organizations from cyber threats. It provides the foundation for proactive cybersecurity strategies, enabling us to anticipate and respond to emerging threats effectively.

The Cyber Threat Intelligence team works closely with our Incident Response (IR) and Security Operations Center (SOC) teams to ensure timely and actionable intelligence, maximizing our ability to detect and disarm cyber adversaries. By analyzing threat trends, actor behavior, and technical indicators, the domain plays a vital role in safeguarding our customers and driving resilience across industries.

As a leader of this domain, you will have access to unique threat data derived from one of Europe’s leading IR teams and monitoring operations across Northern Europe. Your work will directly contribute to shaping how organizations defend against cybercrime.

The Role

As the Head of Cyber Threat Intelligence, you will lead, innovate, and grow our CTI department. You will oversee strategic initiatives, operational excellence, and collaboration across the organization to ensure we remain a leader in cybersecurity.

Your responsibilities will include:

  • Building and leading the team: Shape, coach, and inspire team members to achieve ambitious goals.
  • Truesec CSIRT (CERT) responsibilities: Maintain relationships with FIRST, TF-CSIRT, cert.se, NCSE, and other organizations while ensuring certifications and active contributions to the CERT community.
  • Operational specialist work: Conduct advanced threat analyses, create insightful reports, and present findings to technical and non-technical stakeholders.
  • Entrepreneurship & commercial development: Develop new CTI offerings, package services, set pricing, and drive proactive business opportunities.
  • Collaborate with IR & SOC teams: Provide actionable Threat Intelligence and innovate ways to utilize threat information for improved resilience.
  • Represent the company: Serve as Truesec’s spokesperson in the CTI domain, delivering presentations, engaging with authorities, and sharing insights.

Your Potential Background

You are an experienced cybersecurity professional who thrives in dynamic and fast-paced environments. You have a deep understanding of Cyber Threat Intelligence and are passionate about building teams, solving complex problems, and making an impact. As a leader, you inspire trust, bring out the best in people, and know how to navigate both technical and business landscapes.

You are motivated by driving innovation and staying ahead of the curve in the ever-evolving cybersecurity domain. You are a curious learner, actively tracking new threats, methods, and trends, and you’re ready to take on one of the most strategic roles in Truesec.

Required Skills:

  • Extensive experience in Cyber Threat Intelligence, including analysis methods, sources, and tools.
  • Proven leadership skills, with a track record of building or expanding high-performing teams.
  • Strong business acumen, with the ability to present, sell, and price services effectively.
  • Fluency in Swedish and English (spoken and written).
  • A passion for cybersecurity, with a proactive approach to staying updated on emerging threats and techniques.
  • Excellent communication and interpersonal skills, capable of engaging with technical teams, customers, and external stakeholders.

Bonus Skills

  • Hands-on experience working in the CERT community, including participation in organizations like FIRST and TF-CSIRT.
  • Certifications such as CREST Certified Threat Intelligence Manager, GIAC Cyber Threat Intelligence, CISSP, or similar.
  • Experience collaborating with government authorities and cybersecurity regulators.
  • Familiarity with advanced threat actor profiling and intelligence sharing frameworks.

Due to the sensitivity in our assignments, we require Swedish citizenship, citizenship in an EU country, or a Swedish work permit.

Join Our Mission Toward a Safe Digital Future

If you truly want to make a difference and make our world safer, this is the place for you. At Truesec, you'll get to be at the forefront of fighting cybercrime instead of reading about it in the news.

This is your chance to be part of an exciting journey in a leading cybersecurity company – and learn from some of the best in the field.

Are you ready to team up with the most dedicated experts and lead the charge in securing Europe’s digital landscape?

We are excited to get to know you!

descriptionHtml:
Do you want to be at the forefront of protecting our society against cyber threats? We are looking for a Legal Counsel to team up with the most dedicated team of cyber specialists in our Legal domain.

About Truesec – A Leading Cybersecurity Company

Since 2005, Truesec has been a native cybersecurity company, driven by one single purpose: Creating safety and sustainability in a digital world by preventing cyber breach and minimizing impact.

Over the years, we’ve earned the trust of organizations worldwide and gained a strong reputation internationally. Our team consists of dedicated specialists covering the entire spectrum of cybersecurity – with capabilities in Predict, Prevent, Protect, Respond, and Recover.

Today, we employ around 300 employees – and as cybercrime grows, we need to grow. To support that goal, we are now looking for a Legal Counsel who wants to make a difference in our Legal Team.

Legal & Cyberlaw

Truesec is an expert cybersecurity organization and since 2021, we have expanded our business to include a legal function. Our legal team manages our company’s internal legal matters and also provides advisory services to our customers during assignments that cross country borders, continents, and even time zones. Together, we are defining what legal advisory services in cyberlaw look like now and in the future.

The Role

Truesec is number one in cybersecurity in Sweden – and we want to claim that spot in cyber law, too.

As a member of Truesec Cyber Law, you will work with a wide variety of tasks – from supporting a premiere technical organization on its mission to create a safer digital future to supporting customers with cyber, cybersecurity, and privacy law. You will work in cross-competence teams together with some of the most experienced and talented lawyers, cybersecurity and information security experts, and incident responders.

You will be based in the Stockholm office together with the rest of the Cyber Law team.

Some examples of tasks and roles you will come across as a Cyber Law member are:

  • Group Strategic Partner: We engage with and advise the group Leadership Team on matters regarding, e.g., corporate law, legal risks, and legal/compliance/privacy strategy.
  • Traditional In-House Counsel: We support the group and its business areas with developing legal, compliance, privacy, and governance frameworks; managing legal risks and issues associated with a rapidly growing and internationalizing tech company; negotiating complex commercial agreements; etc. In the more traditional in-house role, you create the legal framework for an innovative and dynamic organization as well as translate legalese for both businesspeople in management and technical experts working on the field, and the other way around.
  • Cyber Law: We advise customers on cyber law, including preparing for personal data breaches, managing risks related to cyber law, and managing and responding to personal data and other legal breaches, including notifying authorities and coordinating global legal teams, advising on crisis communication, acting as crisis manager, negotiating with our customers’ customers to reduce legal and contractual risk during an incident, etc. Together with Truesec’s market-leading Cybersecurity Incident Response Team (CSIRT), or as a standalone service, we provide customers with a unique one-stop-shop for proactive and reactive legal incident response services.

An Example of Your Background

This is a great opportunity for you if you want to be a part of a successful business and participate in defining the cyber law area. If you are self-acting and feel comfortable in most situations, you have a great chance of finding a space for stimulus and growth in a hard-working and fun team. We believe your background includes some parts of the following:

  • University degree, Swedish LL.M.
  • 3-5 years’ experience in M&A and corporate law from a law firm.
  • Regardless of formal background, you should have a strong will to support both internal and external customers, an entrepreneurial drive to create innovative legal services, and an interest in technology and cyber – you should have a strong will to take part in creating something new and unique within the legal and consulting space.
  • A strong ambition and desire to be part of a purpose bigger than yourself

Due to the sensitivity in our assignments, we require Swedish citizenship, citizenship in an EU country, or a Swedish work permit.

Join Our Mission Toward a Safe Digital Future

If you truly want to make a difference and make our world a little bit safer, this is the place for you. At Truesec, you'll get to be at the forefront of fighting cybercrime instead of reading about it in the news. This is your chance to be part of an exciting journey in a leading cybersecurity company - and learn from some of the best in their field.

So, are you ready to team up with the most dedicated team of experts and be part of a meaningful mission? Then we are excited to get to know you!
